commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: feat(skills): added bundled keeper-commander skill (Keeper Security)
target.body: ## Summary The intention of this PR is to introduce the official Keeper commander skill in the Official installation bundle of OpenClaw that provides openclaw ability to interact with Keeper Security password management platform, [Keeper Commander](https://docs.keeper.io/enterprise-guide/commander-cli). Why does this matter now? Keeper Security is a major enterprise password/PAM platform. A bundled skill gives agents safe defaults (tmux for interactive auth, no secrets in chat, confirmation before deletes) instead of ad-hoc CLI usage. Users managing Keeper vaults, enterprise admin, or PAM via agents have no official, security-reviewed workflow, adding the skill in official repo bundle helps user to use a secure-reviewed workflows. We request repository maintainers to add the `skills / keeper-commander` files in official OpenClaw installation bundle. What is the intended outcome? OpenClaw users who rely on Keeper can use Keeper Commander and KSM from the agent without custom skills or unsafe CLI guesswork. - Bundled skill — keeper-commander ships with OpenClaw, so openclaw skills list exposes a vetted Keeper workflow out of the box. - Safe defaults — Agents follow a standard pattern: verify keeper/ksm on PATH, use tmux for interactive auth/MFA, search metadata before reads, confirm deletes, and never paste secrets into chat or workspace files. - Clear routing — The skill steers users to the right path: setup vs runtime secrets (KSM) vs admin/PAM (Commander), with pointers to [Keeper Agent Kit](https://github.com/Keeper-Security/keeper-agent-kit) and [official Keeper docs](https://docs.keeper.io/) instead of inventing commands. - Enterprise parity — Keeper users get a first-class integration experience comparable to existing bundled password-manager skills, maintained in alignment with Keeper Security’s official tooling. What is intentionally out of scope? NA What does success look like? Success means the bundled skill is merged, discoverable, and agents use Keeper safely in real workflows—not just that a markdown file exists. We are focusing on below success parameters ### Merge and discovery - PR is accepted into openclaw/openclaw under skills/keeper-commander/ - openclaw skills list shows keeper-commander with correct gating (keeper/ksm + tmux on PATH) ### Agent behavior (smoke test) A user can ask something like “Is Keeper installed? Am I logged in?” and the agent: - Checks keeper --help / ksm --help and keeper version without inventing install steps - Uses a dedicated tmux session for interactive work - Runs a non-destructive check (e.g. whoami) without dumping secrets - Stops clearly if keeper or tmux is missing ### Security bar - No secrets, tokens, or master passwords in chat, logs, or workspace files - Destructive actions (delete user/record, remove client device) require explicit user confirmation - Runtime secret injection is routed toward KSM patterns, not ad-hoc file writes ### Maintenance model - Skill stays thin: points to [Keeper Agent Kit](https://github.com/Keeper-Security/keeper-agent-kit) and [official docs](https://docs.keeper.io/) rather than duplicating large command catalogs - Keeper Security can update upstream guidance; the OpenClaw bundle needs only occasional alignment PRs ### User outcome - Keeper users get a first-class bundled integration—they don’t need a custom skill or unsafe prompt workarounds to manage vault, admin, or secrets workflows through OpenClaw. What should reviewers focus on? 1. Security and guardrails - This is a secrets-adjacent skill. Check that the instructions: - Never encourage passwords, tokens, or vault values on the command line or in chat - Require tmux for interactive Keeper work (auth/MFA session continuity) - Require user confirmation before delete/remove/clear operations - Prefer metadata search before secret reads; avoid bulk exports or full vault dumps - Route runtime app-secret injection toward KSM, not Commander shell hacks 2. Scope discipline - Confirm the PR is skill-only (markdown + small references): - No gateway/runtime/plugin changes - No new tools or credential storage in OpenClaw - No large vendored command catalogs — only thin pointers to [Keeper Agent Kit](https://github.com/Keeper-Security/keeper-agent-kit) and [official docs](https://docs.keeper.io/) 3. OpenClaw skill format compliance - Valid SKILL.md frontmatter (name, description) - metadata.openclaw if present: correct requires gating (keeper/ksm, tmux), install spec if included - Single-line JSON in metadata per OpenClaw parser rules - References live under references/ and paths in the skill actually exist ## Real behaviour proof - Behaviour addressed: The keeper-commander skill file is able to install and configure keeper commander and able to do vault operations like fetching, injecting and updating secrets from Vault. - Real environment tested: The skill file is tested and vetted against the official commander packages. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Users can test prompts like - Configure keeper commander or KSM - search QAOpenClaw record in Keeper vault - Evidence after fix: **Demo video can be provided upon request.** - Observed result after fix: The keeper commander skill file can successfully fetch, inject and updates secrets securely via Keeper commander. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behaviour change? No Did config, environment, or migration behaviour change? No Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behaviour change? No
target.author: skachhwaha-ks

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
